description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is rough and weathered, with visible chipping and signs of wear, including patches of graffiti and faded paint. A prominent yellow stripe runs horizontally along the length of the barrier, partially covered by grime and markings. The barrier has a trapezoidal cross-section, with a wide, stable base and a narrower top, designed to deflect vehicles and prevent crossing. Both ends of the barrier are blunt and show additional signs of abrasion and use. The overall appearance suggests it has been in service for an extended period, exposed to the elements and urban activity.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with a yellow stripe, graffiti, and visible chipping, designed for urban traffic control.
description_view_0: End view showing the trapezoidal cross-section, rough concrete texture, and some yellow paint on the sides.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length, yellow stripe, faded paint, and surface wear.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, similar to view 0, with visible chipping and yellow paint remnants.
description_view_3: Other side view with graffiti, yellow stripe, and more pronounced surface wear.
